ogTitle: Carey Bay vs Cargo: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Carey Bay and Cargo.
heading: Carey Bay vs Cargo — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Carey Bay, New South Wales and Cargo, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Carey Bay has a lower overall climate risk score (38/100) compared to Cargo (38/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Carey Bay or Cargo safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Carey Bay has a flood risk score of 22/100, while Cargo scores 54/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Carey Bay scores 63/100 for bushfire risk. Cargo scores 31/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Carey Bay or Cargo?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Carey Bay has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.2% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
